goal: Add RLS policies to all migration schema tables — CLAUDE.md compliance

acceptance_criteria.description: RLS enabled on ALL migration.* content tables: engagement, quality_profile, field_mapping, code_mapping, batch, lineage, exception, reconciliation, correction, analyst_decision, gate_transition, notification, risk, event, certification_record, exception_cluster, canonical_members, canonical_salaries, canonical_contributions, stored_calculations, payment_history, reconciliation_pattern. Policy pattern matches CLAUDE.md Section 6: tenant scoping via engagement_members join. The DDL verification test must enumerate every table to catch future additions.

DDL verification test: parses migration SQL file and asserts that every table listed in AC-1 has ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY, FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY, and a CREATE POLICY statement. This test works in -short mode without a database.
All existing migration API tests still pass with RLS enabled — no test relies on cross-tenant data access
Integration test (skipped in -short mode): verifies that a query with tenant A's context cannot read tenant B's engagement data. Guarded by: if testing.Short() { t.Skip('requires database') }

critical: Every migration content table has an RLS policy — no exceptions
RLS policies use current_setting('app.current_tenant_id'), matching the platform's auth middleware pattern
Tables without direct tenant_id column use a join path that is covered by an index
Force row-level security enabled (ALTER TABLE ... FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY)
high: Migration file numbered correctly after the latest existing migration
Existing tests updated to set app.current_tenant_id in test DB context
No performance regression — RLS join paths use indexed columns
medium: Document the RLS policy pattern in a code comment for future migration table additions
